task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the Aerobridge admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Django administration dashboard is visible in the web browser at http://localhost:8000/admin/.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the form to add a new flight operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add flight operation' (or similar) form page is open in the admin panel.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct name for the flight operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Rajasthan Corridor Inspection' is typed into the Name field of the flight operation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent filled out the required details for the flight operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The fields for assigned drone, flight plan, operator, purpose, pilot, and start/end datetimes are populated with valid selections or data in the form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully saved the flight operation record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (typically a green banner at the top of the Django admin page) is visible, stating that the flight operation 'Rajasthan Corridor Inspection' was added successfully.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent did not log out after saving the record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the admin interface with the user still logged in (e.g., a 'Log out' link is visible, and the login screen is not shown).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking and typing within a web browser window, not using terminal commands (like curl or python scripts) to create the record.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine Aerobridge admin panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the actual Django administration panel at localhost:8000, not a text editor or a locally created mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the success message is genuine and not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using browser developer tools (Inspect Element) to manually insert the success banner or alter text on the page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a flight operation named 'Rajasthan Corridor Inspection' in the Aerobridge Django admin panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The admin credentials are 'admin' and 'adminpass123'. The VLM should verify that the flight operation was created with the exact name 'Rajasthan Corridor Inspection' (case-insensitive) and that the agent remained logged in at the end.
